name: Parts Feeder Rest Pose Analysis
description: An automation engineer is designing a vibratory bowl feeder and robotic vision system. Write a Python script to automate 50 simulated drop tests of a rectangular cuboid (dimensions X=0.05m, Y=0.10m, Z=0.20m) onto a flat floor to determine its natural resting poses. The script must be saved to /home/ga/Documents/CoppeliaSim/scripts/run_drop_test.py and use the ZMQ Remote API to perform drops from [0,0,0.5] with random orientations. For each test, wait for the part to settle (velocity < 0.005 m/s or 5 seconds elapsed) and classify the resting face as 'LARGE', 'MEDIUM', or 'SMALL' based on the final Z-height of the center-of-mass. Save the results to /home/ga/Documents/CoppeliaSim/exports/drop_results.csv (columns: drop_id, init_roll, init_pitch, init_yaw, settle_time_s, rest_z_height, rest_face) and summary statistics to /home/ga/Documents/CoppeliaSim/exports/rest_pose_stats.json (fields: total_drops, large_face_count, medium_face_count, small_face_count).
